title: Astragalus suffruticosus
intro: Astragalus suffruticosus, also known as the low-growing milkvetch, is a perennial plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote overall health and well-being, with reported health benefits including enhanced immune function, improved cardiovascular health, and reduced inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus suffruticosus include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which are thought to be responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Astragalus suffruticosus is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, teas, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support immune function and overall health.
taxonomy: Astragalus suffruticosus belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This plant is a member of the genus Astragalus, which consists of over 2,000 species of vetches and milkvetches. Astragalus suffruticosus is a small, shrubby perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a part of the tribe Genisteae, which also includes other legumes like lupines and broom. The classification of Astragalus suffruticosus reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other legume species.

side_effects: Astragalus suffruticosus is generally considered safe, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications. Rare side effects can include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset. The herb may also cause an increased risk of bleeding when taken with blood thinners or antiplatelet medications. Furthermore, individuals with certain medical conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, should consult a healthcare professional before using Astragalus suffruticosus.
